Fig. 1.2 is not to scale. d 0 = .......

parts.answer: d = 7.5 (cm) idea of allow to (just) tip one way then the other way / owtte b / a = 0.14  axes labelled with quantity and unit  appropriate scales (plots occupying at least ½ grid between plotted points)  plots all correct to ½ small square and precise plots  well-judged line and thin line G present and triangle method seen on graph M R in range 140 to 170 and with unit of g r on pivot without mass M , read position of pivot when rule r as near to balance as possible / owtte b calculated from b = 48.7  p 0625/63 May/June 2023
voltmeter in parallel connection with resistor U

V = 1.7 I = 0.42 R = 4.05 / ecf, 3.89, 3.81 consistent to 2 or consistent to 3 significant figures for R statement matching results values used seen, and justification matching statement e.g. within limits of experimental accuracy / owtte rectangle with strike-through arrow only in series connection can obtain continuous set of values / more straightforward to change current / can obtain more values easily
straight line and through origin 0625/63 May/June 2023  = 15(°) ± 1(°) d = 3.5 (cm) no and too close / must be as far apart as possible

all lines fine, straight and in correct relation  = 27(°) ± 2(°) statement matching results j ustification matching statement and quoting values two suitable precautions, e.g.:  thin lines / sharp pencil  view bottom of pins / keep pins upright  ensure pins far apart

difficult to align pins / place pins accurately / pins (too) thick 0625/63 May/June 2023 4 MP1 apparatus: thermometer and stop-watch MP2 method: measure independent variable and measure temperature and time MP3 repeat for new value of independent variable MP4 control variable: any one from:  volume of water  initial temperature MP5 table: columns, with units, for independent variable, temperature, time MP6 analysis: compare readings to see if change in lid thickness produces change in (rate of) temperature change (owtte) plot line graph (with axes specified) MP7 additional point any one from:  at least 5 sets of data taken  repeat for each value of independent variable and take average  nd appropriate control variable stated  description of calculating rate of cooling from results
